blocks.content: In this milestone, you'll navigate and analyze the Azure metrics collected by Cloud Provider Observability. This milestone assumes you installed preconfigured dashboards and alerts in the previous step.

Cloud Provider Observability provides preconfigured dashboards for the Azure services you're monitoring. Each dashboard visualizes metrics for a specific service type, such as performance, usage, and availability.

Use the **Services** tab to open a service dashboard and explore your Azure metrics.

Use available filters to find and click the service you want to view.

For example, select **Azure Virtual Machines** to view Virtual Machine metrics.

If there are multiple instances of the service, select the specific instance you want to view.

For example, you can select a specific instance of an Azure Virtual Machine.

Use dashboard controls to customize your view:

- Select a **time range** using the time picker in the upper right. For example, select **Last 24 hours** to view recent performance.
- Apply **variable filters** to focus on specific instances, resource groups, or subscriptions. For example, select a specific resource group from the **Resource Group** dropdown.

Use panel controls to investigate and analyze metrics:

- To investigate specific metrics using Explore, click **Explore** on any panel.
- To see ML predictions for certain panels, click **Predict**. For more information on this feature and how to enable it, refer to [View predictions](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/monitor-infrastructure/monitor-cloud-provider/azure/azure-alerts/#view-predictions) in the Cloud Provider Observability Azure documentation.
You opened a service dashboard and can customize time range, filters, and panel controls to analyze Azure metrics.

Dashboards provide the following operational insights:

- **Resource utilization patterns**: Identify peak usage times and plan capacity accordingly across Virtual Machines, databases, and storage
- **Performance bottlenecks**: Spot consistently high CPU, memory, storage, or network utilization that may require optimization
- **Cost optimization opportunities**: Find underutilized resources that could be downsized or terminated to reduce Azure spending
- **Availability monitoring**: Track resource health and service uptime across your Azure environment
- **Service dependencies**: Understand relationships between Azure resources and identify potential cascading failure points
These insights help you prioritize capacity, cost, and reliability improvements across your Azure environment.

When at least one alert is **Firing** and Grafana Assistant is enabled on your stack, **Analyze with Assistant** and the row **Analyze** icon appear on the **Alerts** tab. If all alerts show **Normal**, these Assistant controls aren't displayed.
Click the **Alerts** tab to review preconfigured alerts for your Azure services.
Use the **State**, **Health**, **Severity**, and **Service** filters to narrow results as you review the alerts list.
To see a recommendation for alert resolution, click the tooltip in the **Summary** column.
In the **Alert** column, click an alert name to view the dashboard for that service.

You reviewed alert state and can open service dashboards from the **Alerts** tab.

For more information about available dashboards and alerts, refer to [Preconfigured dashboards and alerts for Azure metrics](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/monitor-infrastructure/monitor-cloud-provider/azure/azure-alerts/). To learn more about Grafana Assistant in Cloud Provider Observability, refer to [Analyze alerts and build dashboards with Grafana Assistant](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/monitor-infrastructure/monitor-cloud-provider/grafana-assistant-integration/).
In your final milestone, you'll celebrate completing this Azure monitoring journey and discover additional resources for expanding your observability capabilities.
